macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin falls to six-week low near $73K as ETF outflows intensify
items.summary: Bitcoin slid to around $73,000 and hit a fresh six-week low after losing key support, with reports pointing to heavy ETF outflows and weaker institutional liquidity. Several market recaps also noted that Ethereum and major altcoins were mixed to lower as the broader crypto market struggled.

items.title: FalconX files confidential U.S. IPO paperwork with the SEC
items.summary: Crypto trading firm FalconX submitted a confidential initial public offering filing in the United States, signaling another major crypto-native company moving toward public markets. The company is reportedly targeting a debut later this year, depending on market conditions.

items.title: Paxos wins regulatory approval to clear U.S. securities on-chain
items.summary: Paxos reportedly became the first blockchain-native firm approved by the SEC to clear and settle U.S. securities on-chain. The development is being framed as a significant regulatory milestone for blockchain-based market infrastructure.

items.title: Tether’s U.S.-focused stablecoin USAT posts rapid monthly growth
items.summary: Tether's USAT stablecoin supply reportedly grew more than 500% month over month, reaching about $140.8 million at the end of April. The jump was backed by a reserve report from Deloitte, highlighting strong early adoption.

items.title: SoFi launches bank-issued stablecoin inside consumer app
items.summary: SoFi rolled out SoFiUSD to its 14.7 million members, making it the first U.S. national bank to embed a bank-issued stablecoin directly into a consumer banking app. The announcement was followed by a notable jump in SoFi's share price.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: OpenAI tests richer ChatGPT ad formats and e-commerce features
items.summary: OpenAI is expanding ChatGPT advertising with larger images, customizable call-to-action buttons, and shopping-focused layouts that include pricing and customer reviews. The move signals a bigger push to monetize conversational AI through commerce integrations.

items.title: Google broadens Preferred Sources in AI Overviews and AI Mode
items.summary: Google is letting users mark favored websites so their links receive visible "Preferred" badges in AI-generated search responses. The change reflects continued product changes around AI search and publisher visibility.

items.title: DuckDuckGo sees growth amid backlash to AI-heavy Google search
items.summary: DuckDuckGo reported higher app installs and search activity after Google's move toward AI agents in search. The development highlights user concern about how AI is reshaping web search behavior.

items.title: Demis Hassabis says society may have only a few years to prepare for AGI
items.summary: Google DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis warned that artificial general intelligence could arrive within a few years, with 2029 described as plausible. The remark adds to growing debate over AI timelines and governance readiness.

items.title: Meta launches subscription tiers and new AI-focused plans
items.summary: Meta officially introduced paid subscriptions across Instagram, Facebook, and WhatsApp, while also testing AI-oriented subscription offerings under a broader Meta One brand. The move points to a more aggressive consumer monetization strategy around AI services.

items.title: Anthropic releases Claude Opus 4.8 with stronger workflow controls
items.summary: Anthropic launched Claude Opus 4.8, describing it as a more affordable upgrade aimed at coding, reasoning, financial analysis, and knowledge work. The company also said larger Mythos-class models are expected soon after additional safety work.

items.title: DeepSeek cuts flagship AI model pricing by 75%
items.summary: DeepSeek announced a permanent 75% price reduction for its V4-Pro model, sharply lowering API costs for developers and enterprise users. The move intensifies price competition in the AI model market.

items.title: Google DeepMind hurricane forecasting work shows AI speed and accuracy gains
items.summary: A report on the 2025 Atlantic hurricane season says the National Hurricane Center partnered with Google DeepMind on an experimental AI forecast model that produced fast predictions and strong accuracy. The coverage emphasizes that human forecasters remain essential for interpretation and public warnings.

items.title: AI-enabled networking gains traction at Network X
items.summary: AT&T and Comcast showcased the AI-era network at Network X, underscoring growing industry interest in AI-driven telecom infrastructure. The report suggests customer-facing adoption is still developing.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: 007 First Light becomes IO Interactive’s fastest-selling game
items.summary: IO Interactive said 007 First Light sold 1.5 million copies in its first 24 hours, making it the studio’s fastest-selling release. The game launched on consoles and PC and appears to have had a strong commercial debut.

items.title: Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 4 officially revealed
items.summary: Activision and Infinity Ward officially announced Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 4, with a planned release on PS5, Xbox Series X/S, PC, and Switch 2. The game is notable for skipping PS4 and Xbox One, marking the first Call of Duty to leave that generation behind.

items.title: Call of Duty: Warzone to shut down on last-gen consoles
items.summary: Activision confirmed that Call of Duty: Warzone will end support on last-gen PlayStation and Xbox hardware. The move follows broader franchise changes tied to current-generation platforms.

items.title: Nintendo investigates a game-breaking bug in a new Switch 2 title
items.summary: Nintendo acknowledged reports of a game-breaking bug affecting a recent Switch 2 game and said it is investigating the issue. An update is expected after the company reviews user inquiries.

items.title: Xbox CEO says Game Pass price cuts are working
items.summary: A recent discussion highlighted comments from Xbox leadership that Game Pass price cuts are producing positive results. The remarks came amid broader discussion of Microsoft’s subscription strategy.

items.title: Playstack is reportedly being sold to Fandom
items.summary: A report surfaced that publisher Playstack is being sold to Fandom. If completed, the deal would further expand Fandom’s footprint in the gaming business.

items.title: Xbox schedules summer showcase and Gears of War: E-Day Direct
items.summary: Xbox has scheduled a summer showcase along with a dedicated Gears of War: E-Day Direct for June 7. The announcements set up a major news cycle for Microsoft’s first-party lineup.

items.title: The first three Resident Evil games arrive on Steam
items.summary: The original versions of Resident Evil, Resident Evil 2, Resident Evil 3: Nemesis, and Breath of Fire IV are now available on Steam. The release gives PC players access to the classic versions of these Capcom titles.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.
